campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026 where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also took public comments on various matters and mentioned the upcoming Boogie Festival happening that weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Public hearing on council reports (Government Code Section 3502)
Boogie Festival this weekend
Public comment period on city matters

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — June 3, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council met to decide which sheriff service options to use for the next budget year. Once they pick their preferred approach, they'll give staff direction on next steps for law enforcement services in Saratoga.
keyTopics: Choosing between different sheriff service level options for FY 2026
Setting the city's near-term approach to policing and public safety
Directing staff on implementation based on Council's decision

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 16, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council held a meeting on June 16, 2026, to approve previous meeting minutes and authorize the Town Manager to finalize several agreements. The council approved contract amendments and new service agreements covering legal services, software, and litigation support.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from June 2 and June 8 meetings
Fifth Amendment to an existing agreement
Five-year software services contract
Fourth Amendment to legal services contract
New litigation legal services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 17, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to prepare upcoming agendas and handle administrative items. They reviewed the June 23rd final agenda, approved city spending for the Rose, White & Blue Parade and Festival, and processed public records from early June.
keyTopics: Finalized the June 23, 2026 City Council agenda
Approved city funds and donations for the Annual Rose, White & Blue Parade and Festival
Reviewed public records from June 4-11, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to handle routine business including budget planning for the next fiscal year, water management updates, and several construction projects. The council also honored local institutions and a city official's judicial appointment, while addressing two pending legal cases that pose financial risk to the city.
keyTopics: Two pending lawsuits with significant financial exposure to the city
Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget and spending limits
Water management plan updates and drought contingency planning
Capital improvement projects including Public Safety Building and golf course parking lot repaving
City Attorney Jennifer Logue's appointment to the judiciary

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting at 7 P.M. The closed session portion means specific details about what was discussed are not publicly available, as these meetings are held in private for personnel, legal, or other confidential matters.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held (specific topics not publicly disclosed)
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a study session to review how parks and recreational facilities are currently designated and zoned in Cupertino, and to ensure the city is following state laws that protect these public spaces. The meeting also included a public comment period where residents could address the Council on topics not listed on the agenda.
keyTopics: Review of current zoning for parks and recreational facilities
Corrections to existing park and parkland designations
Compliance with state law protections for public recreation areas
Public comment period for non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a hybrid meeting on June 30, 2026, allowing residents to participate both in-person and remotely via Zoom. The city provided multiple ways for the public to submit comments and stay informed about council decisions.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation options via Zoom video conference
Phone dial-in access for those unable to use video
Written public comment submission through eComment system

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — June 16, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas City Council met to approve funding and development plans for the city. The council adopted a community development funding plan for 2026-2027, approved a new residential or commercial development project, and reviewed a new crime prevention program for storefronts.
keyTopics: 2026-2027 Community Development Block Grant funding approved
New site development project approved with density permits
Storefront Crime Prevention Program under review
City council meeting schedule and minutes approved

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve turning part of the Measure E development site into public parkland and to review plans for expanding the driving range at Baylands Golf Links. The council also received updates from liaisons and departments on various city matters.
keyTopics: New park dedication on Measure E site
Baylands Golf Links driving range expansion feasibility
Council liaison and department reports
